evals.prompt: I just added a new package at pkg/caching/ that provides Redis-backed cache abstraction with TTL support, cache-aside pattern, and batch invalidation. It also has a CachingManager that wraps go-redis. Can you create the documentation for it in docs_wiki/ as a new file called CACHING.md? Make sure it matches the style and conventions of the existing docs (like RESILIENCE.md). Also update docs_wiki/README.md to add it to the Package Deep Dives table. The config key is 'caching', and it depends on the redis infrastructure component.
evals.expected_output: New docs_wiki/CACHING.md file created + docs_wiki/README.md updated with new table entry
expectations: Creates docs_wiki/CACHING.md with proper H1 title and summary
Uses code blocks with language tags for Go examples
Follows the package deep-dive pattern (sub-features, usage examples, best practices)
Adds a row to the Package Deep Dives table in README.md with package path pkg/caching/
Uses the project's module path (stackyrd-nano) in imports

evals.prompt: I added the request_id middleware to internal/middleware/request_id.go. It generates a unique X-Request-ID for each incoming request and propagates it to downstream services. The config key is 'request_id' under middleware in config.yaml. Can you update the middleware table in docs_wiki/REFERENCE.md to include the new middleware? The table lists middleware with columns: Name, Config Key, Purpose. Also check if docs_wiki/README.md needs any updates — the Middleware doc table might list the middleware entries.
evals.expected_output: Updated REFERENCE.md middleware table and README.md if needed
expectations: Adds request_id row to the middleware table in REFERENCE.md
Uses correct column format matching existing table (Name, Config Key, Purpose)
Keeps the table alphabetically sorted or consistently ordered with existing entries
Does not modify any other part of REFERENCE.md unnecessarily
Updates README.md if the middleware table there is also affected

evals.prompt: Can you update docs_wiki/DEVELOPMENT.md to add a new section about using the cache? It should go after the 'Using Plugins from Services' section. The caching package is at pkg/caching/ and provides a CachingManager that wraps go-redis with cache-aside pattern, TTL support, and batch invalidation. The config key is 'caching' under services in config.yaml. Write a section called 'Using the Cache' with a code example showing how to inject it via Dependencies and use the cache-aside pattern.
evals.expected_output: Updated docs_wiki/DEVELOPMENT.md with new 'Using the Cache' section
expectations: Adds a new '## Using the Cache' section after the 'Using Plugins from Services' section
Includes a Go code example using the module path stackyrd-nano/pkg/caching
Code example shows getting the CachingManager from deps (deps.Get)
Shows the cache-aside pattern (check cache, miss, load from DB, store in cache)
Mentions the config toggle services.caching in config.yaml
Table of contents / section ordering is preserved correctly
